scripts.build: yarn sync && tsc && vite build --mode production
scripts.//deploy.intent: Publish the React app to the ROOT of gh-pages, leaving /fonts/ (owned by .github/workflows/build-fonts.yml) untouched, and keep the branch lean by force-pushing a single commit per deploy.
scripts.//deploy.no-history: `--no-history` force-pushes one commit per deploy. The previous setup accumulated months of hashed Vite bundles (assets/index-XXXX.js, etc.) because the old `--add` flag preserved everything, including stale builds — gh-pages branch grew to >500 files and the auto pages-build-deployment workflow took several minutes just to checkout. Single-commit history keeps the branch tree size = current snapshot, forever.
scripts.//deploy.remove: `--remove '{assets,wingfont}/**'` is what replaces `--add`. The default gh-pages CLI behavior with no `--add` is `--remove '.'` (wipe the whole branch), which would clobber the /fonts/ that build-fonts.yml just deployed. By overriding `--remove` to ONLY the two subdirs we actually own (assets/ for Vite's hashed bundles, wingfont/ for the Python sources mirror copied by sync-python.mjs), we get a clean replace of web-owned content while /fonts/ survives untouched. Root-level files (index.html, .nojekyll, CNAME, vite.svg, favicon.ico) have stable filenames and are simply overwritten by the copy step.
scripts.//deploy.dotfiles: `--dotfiles` is still required so `.nojekyll` (which lives in public/ and is copied to dist/.nojekyll by Vite) actually ships to gh-pages — without it, GitHub Pages runs Jekyll, which silently strips every file starting with `_` from the served site, including the Python package init files (`__init__.py`) that the Pyodide worker fetches at runtime.
